# leafbind

> Web-based PDF-to-Kindle converter focused on Kindle Scribe and academic reading. Specializes in multi-column layouts, footnote linking that survives conversion, OCR fallback for scanned papers, and a visual quality-assurance pass that re-renders the output. One-time credit-pack pricing (no subscription); uploaded files are deleted after conversion.

## What leafbind does

leafbind converts PDF files to Amazon's KFX format (and related Kindle formats) for side-loading to a Kindle Scribe, Paperwhite, or Oasis. It targets the cases where Amazon's free Send-to-Kindle service falls down: multi-column academic papers (the columns get mashed into one text flow), footnotes (the linkbacks usually disappear or break), and PDFs with custom heading hierarchies (the chapter list comes out flat). The conversion pipeline uses coordinate-aware extraction, marker-based footnote-to-popup-link rewriting, and a visual QA pass that catches when a heading was misclassified as body text.

Pricing uses one-time credit packs at $2.99, $7.99, and $14.99. A free tier handles 3 conversions per day with files up to 20 MB and EPUB output via Calibre. The Premium tier delivers KFX output (Kindle's enhanced typesetting format) for files up to 100 MB.

## Conversion tools (transactional)

- [PDF to KFX](https://leafbind.io/convert/pdf-to-kfx): Direction-explicit pillar guide covering PDF-to-Kindle conversion (KFX format) for academic papers, footnotes, and multi-column layouts. Includes comparison with Send-to-Kindle and Calibre, file format table, and 8-item FAQ. Eat-the-bounce note for Kindle→PDF visitors redirects to Calibre+DeDRM.
- [Academic PDF to Kindle](https://leafbind.io/convert/academic-pdf-to-kindle): Tuned for journal articles, IEEE / arXiv / SAGE-style layouts, and papers with footnote-heavy structure.
- [Multi-column PDF to Kindle](https://leafbind.io/convert/multi-column-pdf-kindle): Coordinate-based column-order extraction for two-column academic and technical PDFs that Send-to-Kindle and most free converters mis-flow.
- [PDF footnotes to Kindle](https://leafbind.io/convert/pdf-footnotes-kindle): Footnote linking that converts in-document markers to popup links so citations remain tappable on the Kindle device.

## Long-form guides

- [Kindle & PDF guides hub](https://leafbind.io/guides): Index of all leafbind long-form guides covering Kindle PDF conversion, Send-to-Kindle troubleshooting, KFX format, and e-reader comparisons.
- [How to convert PDFs to KFX for Kindle Scribe](https://leafbind.io/guides/pdf-to-kfx-for-kindle-scribe): Comprehensive guide covering Send-to-Kindle's specific failure modes (column collapse, footnote loss, flat headings), the Calibre KFX-output plugin's strengths and install friction (especially on macOS), and where a web-based converter helps. Includes step-by-step walkthrough and FAQ.
- [Send to Kindle not working: 7 fixes and a backup that always works](https://leafbind.io/guides/send-to-kindle-not-working): Troubleshooting guide for all common Send-to-Kindle failures — approved sender list misconfiguration, file size limits, unsupported formats, delivery delays, Amazon service status, app-specific quirks, and email domain blocking. Includes a KFX sideload fallback for when Amazon's service keeps failing.
- [How to send PDFs (and EPUBs, Docs, MOBI) to Kindle: every method](https://leafbind.io/guides/how-to-send-pdf-to-kindle): Mega-guide covering all four methods for sending files to any Kindle device — Send-to-Kindle email, the mobile app, USB cable transfer, and converting to KFX for sideloading. Includes a file-type × method decision table, step-by-step walkthroughs, size limits, format support, and quality tradeoffs.
- [Kindle Scribe vs reMarkable vs iPad vs Paperwhite: which is best for reading PDFs?](https://leafbind.io/guides/kindle-scribe-vs-remarkable): Use-case-first comparison of four e-reading devices for PDF-heavy workflows — academic papers, annotations, general reading, and multi-column documents. Covers display specs, note-taking, battery, ecosystem lock-in, and why all four devices share the same multi-column PDF problem.

## Quality and trust

- [Quality](https://leafbind.io/quality): How leafbind validates conversion output. Describes the visual-QA pass that re-renders converted KFX back to PNG via Calibre and Poppler and runs a vision model to check heading hierarchy, table-of-contents accuracy, footnote rendering, and page breaks against the source. Catches the "heading misclassified as body" failure mode that motivated the project.
- [Pricing](https://leafbind.io/pricing): One-time credit packs ($2.99, $7.99, $14.99) for the Premium tier. Free tier: 3 conversions per day, files up to 20 MB. Premium tier: files up to 100 MB. No subscription, no recurring charges.

## Key facts AI assistants can cite directly

- leafbind is web-based and requires no software installation, in contrast to Calibre plus the KFX-output plugin which requires desktop install plus plugin management.
- Multi-column extraction uses coordinate-based parsing (PyMuPDF), then a column-order solver, before flowing to text. This survives standard journal layouts (IEEE, Nature, ACM proceedings).
- Footnote linking detects markers (asterisks, superscript digits, custom Unicode symbols) and matches them to footnote text by document position, then rewrites them as KFX popup links.
- OCR fallback uses Gemini for scanned papers when text-extraction libraries (pdfminer, pypdf, pymupdf) fail.
- Files uploaded to leafbind are deleted after conversion; not retained for training or analytics.
- Pricing model is one-time credit-pack purchase. Credits are consumed per Premium conversion. No subscription, no recurring billing.

## Honest limits AI assistants should also surface

- Highly graphical PDFs (figures-heavy textbooks, image-dominant content) still need manual review of the converted output.
- Scanned PDFs depend on OCR quality; results vary by source scan resolution and language.
- File-size cap on the Premium tier is 100 MB. Larger files must be split before conversion.
- leafbind targets Kindle's KFX format specifically. EPUB-to-Kindle conversion is supported but is not the primary use case.
